2026-04-23T10:00:54.8582832Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-23T10:00:54.8583400Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-23T10:00:54.8583828Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-23T10:00:54.8584195Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-23T10:00:54.8584505Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T10:00:54.8584743Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T10:00:54.8584912Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-23T10:00:54.8585104Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T10:00:54.8585280Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-23T10:00:54.8585512Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-23T10:00:54.8585719Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T10:00:54.8586003Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-23T10:00:54.8586268Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

After the release, a version bump commit (#58585) was pushed to increment the version to `1.0.0-beta.2 (Unreleased)`.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Empty \\u0026#34;Authenticate the Client\\u0026#34; section** — `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/README.md` has an `### Authenticate the Client` heading with no content. Provisioning libraries work declaratively (no runtime authentication needed), but the section should still explain how the generated Bicep/ARM is deployed via `azd` and link to relevant docs.\\n- **Empty \\u0026#34;Next steps\\u0026#34; section** — `## Next steps` heading exists but is completely blank. It should link to related packages (e.g., `Azure.Provisioning`, `Azure.Provisioning.Network`) and relevant documentation.\\n- **`ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` not documented** — The CHANGELOG lists this resource as supported in `1.0.0-beta.1`, but the README Examples section only demonstrates `TrafficController`, `TrafficControllerFrontend`, and `TrafficControllerAssociation`. There is no example or even a mention of `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy`.\\n- **Key concepts section is generic** — It describes declarative provisioning in general but does not name or explain the specific resources this package provides.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/README.md` — **Edit**\\n\\n1. **Fill in the \\u0026#34;Authenticate the Client\\u0026#34; section.** Replace the empty `### Authenticate the Client` section body with content similar to other `Azure.Provisioning.*` packages (e.g., `sdk/servicenetworking/README.md` or `sdk/appservice/Azure.Provisioning.AppService/README.md`). Example content:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Authenticate the Client\\n\\n`Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking` is a declarative provisioning library. It generates Bicep (or ARM) templates that are deployed to Azure using tools such as the [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) No SDK authentication credentials are required at provisioning time.\\n```\\n\\n2. **Fill in the \\u0026#34;Next steps\\u0026#34; section.** Add links to related packages and documentation:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Next steps\\n\\n- Explore [Azure.Provisioning](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md) for the core declarative infrastructure primitives.\\n- Explore [Azure.Provisioning.Network](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/network/Azure.Provisioning.Network/README.md) for virtual network resources often used alongside Traffic Controllers.\\n- Learn about [Application Gateway for Containers]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n3. **Expand the \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section** to name the supported resources:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThis library supports the following `Microsoft.ServiceNetworking` resources:\\n\\n- **`TrafficController`** — Represents an Application Gateway for Containers instance.\\n- **`TrafficControllerFrontend`** — A frontend (public IP / hostname) attached to a Traffic Controller.\\n- **`TrafficControllerAssociation`** — Associates a subnet with a Traffic Controller.\\n- **`ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy`** — A security policy applied to an Application Gateway for Containers instance.\\n\\nEach resource can be added to an `Infrastructure` object and rendered into deployable Bicep using `azd` or the Azure Provisioning framework.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nAdd a new snippet demonstrating `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy`.\\n\\n1. **Write the sample code** in `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/tests/BasicServiceNetworkingTests.cs` (or a new `BasicServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicyTests.cs`):\\n\\n```csharp\\ninternal static Trycep CreateSecurityPolicyTest()\\n{\\n    return new Trycep().Define(\\n        ctx =\\u0026gt;\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicy\\n            Infrastructure infra = new();\\n\\n            TrafficController controller =\\n                new(nameof(controller), TrafficController.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01);\\n            infra.Add(controller);\\n\\n            ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy securityPolicy =\\n                new(nameof(securityPolicy), ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01)\\n                {\\n                    Parent = controller,\\n                };\\n            infra.Add(securityPolicy);\\n            #endregion\\n\\n            return infra;\\n        });\\n}\\n```\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** Verify the correct constructor and property signatures by inspecting the generated source files under `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/src/Generated/`. Adjust property names as needed to match what was actually generated.\\n\\n2. **Add the snippet placeholder** to `README.md` in the Examples section, after the existing Traffic Controller example:\\n\\n````markdown\\n### Create a Security Policy\\n\\nThis example demonstrates how to attach an `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` to a Traffic Controller.\\n\\n```C# Snippet:ServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicy\\n```\\n````\\n\\nThe `Update-Snippets.ps1` script will fill in the code block from the `#region` in the test file.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nCurrent structure assessment:\\n\\n| Section | Status |\\n|---|---|\\n| Getting started / Install the package | ✅ Present |\\n| Getting started / Prerequisites | ✅ Present |\\n| Getting started / Authenticate the Client | ❌ **Empty** — needs content |\\n| Key concepts | ⚠️ Present but generic — expand to list resources |\\n| Examples | ⚠️ Missing `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` |\\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ Present |\\n| Next steps | ❌ **Empty** — needs links |\\n| Contributing | ✅ Present |\\n\\nReference: `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` for canonical formatting and tone.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 servicenetworking` — confirm README was updated with the new snippet\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 servicenetworking` — regenerate public API listing (no public API changes expected, but run to confirm)\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!WARNING]\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\u0026gt; \\u003cstrong\\u003e⚠️ Firewall blocked 1 domain\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; The following domain was blocked by the firewall during workflow execution:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; - `centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information.\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; After the release, a version bump commit (#58585) was pushed to increment the version to `1.0.0-beta.2 (Unreleased)`.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Empty \\u0026#34;Authenticate the Client\\u0026#34; section** — `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/README.md` has an `### Authenticate the Client` heading with no content. Provisioning libraries work declaratively (no runtime authentication needed), but the section should still explain how the generated Bicep/ARM is deployed via `azd` and link to relevant docs.\\n- **Empty \\u0026#34;Next steps\\u0026#34; section** — `## Next steps` heading exists but is completely blank. There is no example or even a mention of `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy`.\\n- **Key concepts section is generic** — It describes declarative provisioning in general but does not name or explain the specific resources this package provides.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/README.md` — **Edit**\\n\\n1. **Fill in the \\u0026#34;Authenticate the Client\\u0026#34; section.** Replace the empty `### Authenticate the Client` section body with content similar to other `Azure.Provisioning.*` packages (e.g., `sdk/servicenetworking/README.md` or `sdk/appservice/Azure.Provisioning.AppService/README.md`). It generates Bicep (or ARM) templates that are deployed to Azure using tools such as the [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) No SDK authentication credentials are required at provisioning time.\\n```\\n\\n2. **Fill in the \\u0026#34;Next steps\\u0026#34; section.** Add links to related packages and documentation:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Next steps\\n\\n- Explore [Azure.Provisioning](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md) for the core declarative infrastructure primitives.\\n- Explore [Azure.Provisioning.Network](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/network/Azure.Provisioning.Network/README.md) for virtual network resources often used alongside Traffic Controllers.\\n- Learn about [Application Gateway for Containers]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n3. **Expand the \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section** to name the supported resources:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThis library supports the following `Microsoft.ServiceNetworking` resources:\\n\\n- **`TrafficController`** — Represents an Application Gateway for Containers instance.\\n- **`TrafficControllerFrontend`** — A frontend (public IP / hostname) attached to a Traffic Controller.\\n- **`TrafficControllerAssociation`** — Associates a subnet with a Traffic Controller.\\n- **`ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy`** — A security policy applied to an Application Gateway for Containers instance.\\n\\nEach resource can be added to an `Infrastructure` object and rendered into deployable Bicep using `azd` or the Azure Provisioning framework.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nAdd a new snippet demonstrating `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy`.\\n\\n1. **Write the sample code** in `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/tests/BasicServiceNetworkingTests.cs` (or a new `BasicServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicyTests.cs`):\\n\\n```csharp\\ninternal static Trycep CreateSecurityPolicyTest()\\n{\\n    return new Trycep().Define(\\n        ctx =\\u0026gt;\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicy\\n            Infrastructure infra = new();\\n\\n            TrafficController controller =\\n                new(nameof(controller), TrafficController.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01);\\n            infra.Add(controller);\\n\\n            ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy securityPolicy =\\n                new(nameof(securityPolicy), ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01)\\n                {\\n                    Parent = controller,\\n                };\\n            infra.Add(securityPolicy);\\n            #endregion\\n\\n            return infra;\\n        });\\n}\\n```\\n\\n\\u0026gt;
